Lesson 6

Questions 1001–1200 · Tap a card to reveal the explanation.

1

Who is the current Finance minister of KPK _______?

Correct Answer: Muzamil Aslam
Show explanation
Explanation

Muzamil Aslam is a Pakistani politician who is the Provincial Minister of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa for Finance. 

Party affilitaion: PTI

Appointed in March 2024


Last updated on 28-03-2026

Click here for reference 

2

Which provincial government announced the implementation of a "watermarking system" on exam papers to curb cheating in 2026?

Correct Answer: Sindh
Show explanation
Explanation

The Sindh government announced the implementation of a modern "watermarking system" on exam papers to curb cheating, prevent paper leaks, and trace culprits in 2026.

The watermarking system allows officials to immediately identify the source of leaked papers.

 

Additional Information:  

The system will be applied to matriculation (9th/10th) and intermediate (11th/12th) examinations scheduled to start in April 2026 across various boards in Sindh.

****

3

Who is the current Education minister of Balochistan?

Correct Answer: Raheela Durrani
Show explanation
Explanation

Raheela Durrani is the current Education  Minister of Balochistan. 

Raheela Durrani appointed on 20 April 2024.

Belonging: Quetta

Party affiliation: Pakistan Muslim League (N)



Last updated on 25-03-2026





4

Who wins the gold for Pakistan at World Beach Wrestling 2021?

Correct Answer: Inam Butt
Show explanation
Explanation

Inam Butt

  • Event: Beach Wrestling World Series 2021, held in Greece.
  • Category: 90kg weight class.
  • Final Match: Defeated Azerbaijan’s Ibrahim Yusubov.
  • Score: 3-0 in the final, September 2021.
  • Achievement: Secured second consecutive gold in 2021, having previously won in Rome, Italy.

Last verified on 24-03-2026

5

What is Pakistan's rank in Miserable Countries Ranking 2018?

Correct Answer: 34
Show explanation
Explanation
  • According to the Miserable Countries Ranking 2018, Pakistan was ranked 34th.
  • Venezuela was the number 1 most miserable country in 2018.

Last verified on 24-03-2026

6

Which woman from Pakistan has been named in BBC's 100 Women of 2019?

Correct Answer: Jalila Haider
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Jalila Haider: Pakistani human rights activist and lawyer.
  • Recognition: Named in BBC’s 100 Women of 2019 for her work in women’s and minority rights.

Last verified on 24-03-2026

7

Which Chief Justice of Peshawar High Court inaugurated two child protection courts in Mardan and Abbottabad districts on October 5th 2019?

Correct Answer: Justice Waqar Ahmed Seth
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Event Date: 5 October 2019
  • Courts Inaugurated: Child Protection Courts in Mardan and Abbottabad districts.
  • Purpose: To provide specialized judicial mechanisms for protecting children’s rights.
  • Chief Justice: Justice Waqar Ahmed Seth of Peshawar High Court.

Last verified on 24-03-2026

8

Who was the served as Chairman of Strategic Policy Planning Cell (SPPC)?

Correct Answer: Dr. Moeed Yusuf
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Appointment: Chairman of SPPC under National Security Division, Pakistan (2019–2021).
  • Concurrent Role: Special Assistant to the Prime Minister on National Security Division and Strategic Policy Planning.
  • SPPC Function: Advises on national security, strategic policy, and planning.

Last verified on 24-03-2026

9

Who has been elected as a member of the Executive Committee of the Inter-Parliamentary Union in 1919?

Correct Answer: Raza Rabbani
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Raza Rabbani: Elected to IPU Executive Committee in October 2019.
  • Event: 141st General Assembly held in Belgrade.
  • Term: Representing Pakistan for 3 years.
  • Aqeel Malik: In April 2025, elected to IPU High-Level Advisory Group on Countering Terrorism & Violent Extremism.

Last verified on 24-03-2026

10

Who introduced the 8th Amendment in 1973 Constitution of Pakistan?

Correct Answer: General Zia-ul-Haq
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Passed By: National Assembly in November 1985 under PM Muhammad Khan Junejo.
  • Drafted/Enforced By: To legalize actions of General Zia-ul-Haq’s military regime (1977–1985).
  • Purpose: Shifted Pakistan from a parliamentary democracy to a quasi-presidential system.
  • Key Feature: President gained the power to dissolve the National Assembly under Article 58(2)(b).
  • Result: Legalized all martial law orders, ordinances, and actions of Zia, strengthening the President’s authority over the Prime Minister.

Last verified on 24-03-2026

11

In Pakistan the monsoon season lasts from ____?

Correct Answer: July to September
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Monsoon season in Pakistan starts in July and ends in September.
  • It bringing significant rainfall mainly to northern and eastern regions. 

Additional information:

  • Cool, dry winter: December to February
  • Hot, dry spring: March to May
  • Summer rainy season (southwest monsoon): June to September
  • Retreating monsoons: October to November

Last verified on 23-03-2026

12

Which city of Pakistan receives highest annual rainfall?

Correct Answer: Murree
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Murree receives the highest rainfall in all of Pakistan with an annual average of more than 1500 mm.
  • The area having the lowest rainfall in Pakistan is Nokhudi (Balochistan)
  • Equatorial regions receive rainfall throughout the year.

Last verified on 23-03-2026

13

In constitution 1973 what number of seats in Senate was set?

Correct Answer: 100 Seats
Show explanation
Explanation

Initial Senate seats (1973): 100

Later changes:

  • Increased to 104 seats (including FATA & minorities)
  • After reforms (25th Amendment), adjusted again
  • Current structure: around 96 seats (after FATA merger)

Term of Senators: 6 years

Half members retire every: 3 years

  • Originally, the membership of Senate was a were 45 that was raised to 63, in 1977 and 87 in 1985.

Last verified on 23-03-2026
14

When NADRA was established?

Correct Answer: March 10, 2000
Show explanation
Explanation
  • NADRA stands for National Database and Registration Authority.
  • It was established on 10 March 2000.
  • Headquarters: Islamabad 
  • The largest NADRA office is situated in Islamabad.
  • Formal name: National Database Organization (NDO), an attached department under the Ministry of Interior, Government of Pakistan, in 1998.

Last verified on 23-03-2026

15

The Battle of Miani was fought between Britain and ______?

Correct Answer: Talpur
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The Battle of Miani was fought on 17 February 1843.
  • It was between the British East India Company and the Talpur rulers of Sindh.
  • Sir Charles Napier led the British army and defeated the Talpurs.
  • It leading to the annexation of Sindh.

Last verified on 14-03-2026

16

The Potohar Plateau is located between which two rivers?

Correct Answer: Sindh and Jhelum
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Potohar Plateau is a plateau in northern Punjab, Pakistan.
  • It is known for its undulating terrain and archaeological sites.
  • It is situated between the rivers Sindh (Indus) and Jhelum.
  • It is forming a natural plateau region.

Last verified on 19-03-2026

17

The people of Mohenjo-Daro did not worship which of the following? Bull, Inanna, Jagauri, Mother Earth

Correct Answer: Inanna
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Mohenjo-Daro was a major city of the Indus Valley Civilization (c. 2500–1900 BCE) in present-day Pakistan.
  • People worshiped Mother Earth, bulls, and fertility symbols.
  • But Inanna (a Mesopotamian goddess) was not part of their religion.

Last verified on 19-03-2026

18

When did British conquer Sindh?

Correct Answer: 1843
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The British conquered Sindh in 1843 after their victory in the Battle of Miani.
  • The annexation of Sindh to British India was formalized through the signing of the Treaty of Annexation in 1847.

Additional information:

Timeline of Sindh Conquests / Rule

  • 1026 AD: Sindh conquered by Sultan Mehmood Ghaznavi
  • 1050 AD: Soomras take control from Ghaznavides
  • 1351 AD: Samas take control from Soomras
  • 1520–1566 AD: Rule of Sultan Suleiman the Magnificent
  • 1520–1591 AD: Sindh ruled by Shah Baig Argoon, Shah Hassan Argoon, Mirza Isa Tarkhan, etc.
  • 1591 AD: Sindh conquered by Mughals
  • 1701 AD: Kalhoras take control from Mughals
  • 1783 AD: Talpurs overthrow Kalhoras
  • 17 Feb 1843: Sindh annexed by British from Talpurs

Last verified on 16-05-2026


19

Which country lies in the north of Pakistan?

Correct Answer: China
Show explanation
Explanation
  • China — North‑northeast (around 559–595 km)
  • Afghanistan — Northwest (~2,400 km+)
  • India — East (~2,900 km+)
  • Iran — West (~900 km)
  • Arabian Sea — South (~1,046 km)

Last verified on 19-03-2026

20

Pakistan conducted airstrikes in Afghanistan under which operation in 2026?

Correct Answer: Operation Ghazab-ul-Haq
Show explanation
Explanation

In 2026, Afghanistan launched a retaliatory operation against Pakistan. In response, Pakistan launched Operation Ghazab Lil Haq against the Taliban. 


Additional Information:

This operation was initiated following an escalation in border skirmishes and cross-border firing incidents along the Durand Line, involving the Afghan Taliban and Pakistani security forces.The following day, Pakistan announced a state of "open war" with Afghanistan.

 ****

21

Which of the following is a non-renewable resource used for energy production in Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Coal
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Coal is a non-renewable resource because it takes millions of years to form and cannot be replenished quickly.
  • Pakistan uses coal for energy production, especially in power plants like the Thar coal project.

Non-renewable energy sources:

  • Oil
  • Nuclear energy
  • Petroleum
  • Natural gas & coal

Renewable sources of energy:

  • Solar energy
  • Wind energy
  • Hydroelectric power
  • Biomass energy
  • Geothermal energy
  • Tidal energy

Last verified on 11-02-2026

22

Abdullah Haroon, a leader of the Khilafat Movement and Sindh Muslim League, first joined which political party in India?

Correct Answer: Indian National Congress
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Abdullah Haroon (1872–1942) was a prominent Muslim leader of Sindh.
  • Initial party joined: Indian National Congress
  • Later joined: All-India Muslim League
  • Movement involvement: Khilafat Movement (1919–1924)

Last verified on 18-03-2026

23

In which year did the Talpurs take power from the Kalhoras?

Correct Answer: 1783
Show explanation
Explanation

Timeline of Sindh Conquests / Rule

  • 1026 AD: Sindh conquered by Sultan Mehmood Ghaznavi
  • 1050 AD: Soomras take control from Ghaznavides
  • 1351 AD: Samas take control from Soomras
  • 1520–1566 AD: Rule of Sultan Suleiman the Magnificent
  • 1520–1591 AD: Sindh ruled by Shah Baig Argoon, Shah Hassan Argoon, Mirza Isa Tarkhan, etc.
  • 1591 AD: Sindh conquered by Mughals
  • 1701 AD: Kalhoras take control from Mughals
  • 1783 AD: Talpurs overthrow Kalhoras
  • 17 Feb 1843: Sindh annexed by British from Talpurs

Last verified on 16-05-2026

24

Who was the first Pakistani woman cricketer to score a century in the Women’s Cricket World Cup 2022?

Correct Answer: Muneeba Ali
Show explanation
Explanation

Muneeba Ali

  • Runs scored: 102
  • Balls faced: 68 (strike rate ~150)
  • Tournament: ICC Women’s T20 World Cup 2022
  • Opponent: Ireland
  • Venue: Newlands, Cape Town, South Africa
  • Milestone: First T20I century by a Pakistani woman

Last verified on 18-03-2026

25

Which place is called the “Roof of the World”?

Correct Answer: Tibet
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Tibet is called the “Roof of the World”.
  • It is located on the Tibetan Plateau in Asia, surrounded by the Himalayas to the south.
  • Tibet is often called the “Third Pole” because of its large ice fields.

Additional information:

  • Average elevation: ~4,500 meters (14,800 ft)
  • Highest peak nearby: Mount Everest (8,848 meters)
  • Area of Tibetan Plateau: ~2.5 million km²
  • Location: Central Asia
  • Major rivers originating: Yangtze, Mekong, Indus, Brahmaputra

Last verified on 18-03-2026

26

On which day is the first atomic energy experiment commemorated?

Correct Answer: Youm-e-Takbeer
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Youm-e-Takbeer is celebrated on May 28 every year in Pakistan.
  • Marks the first atomic energy experiment and nuclear tests: Chagai-I and Chagai-II.
  • Made Pakistan the seventh nuclear state in the world.

Last verified on 17-03-2026

27

Which is the largest fort of Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Ranikot Fort
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Ranikot Fort is located in Jamshoro District, Sindh.
  • It is also known as The Great Wall of Sindh.
  • It is believed to be the world's largest fort.
  • It is circumference of approximately 32 KM or 20 Mile.
  • The site was nominated in 1993 by the Pakistan National Commission for UNESCO World Heritage status.
  • The fort is listed as a historical site under the Antiquities Act, 1975.
  • The small Fort within Rani Kot Fort is known as Mari Kot.

Last verified on 17-03-2026

28

The Pakistan Navy Headquarters is located in ______?

Correct Answer: Islamabad
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The Pakistan Navy is the naval branch of the Pakistan Armed Forces.
  • Headquarters: Islamabad
  • Founded: August 14, 1947
  • Gifford James was the first C-in-C of the Pakistan Navy.
  • Admiral Mohammad Siddique was the first Muslim C-in-C of the Pakistan Navy.
  • The Chief of the Naval Staff, a four-star admiral, commands the navy.

Last verified on 17-03-2026

29

Who was the second Prime Minister of Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Kh. Nazim-ud-Din
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Khawaja Nazimuddin served as the 2nd Prime Minister of Pakistan from 17 October 1951 to 17 April 1953.
  • Predecessor: Liaquat Ali Khan
  • Successor: Mohammad Ali Bogra
  • Born: July 1894, Dacca (now Dhaka, Bangladesh)
  • Died: 22 October 1964, Dacca
  • Burial: Mausoleum of the Three Leaders, Dhaka

List of PM of Pakistan

1Mr. Liaqat Ali Khan15-08-1947 To 16-10-1951

30

Who Mughal emperor built Shalimar garden?

Correct Answer: Shah Jehan
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Shalimar Gardens in Lahore was built by Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan.
  • Construction Year: 1641–42
  • Included in UNESCO World Heritage Site: 1981
  • Terraces: 3 main terraces (upper, middle, lower)
  • Water Fountains: Over 400 fountains
  • Total Area: Approximately 16 hectares
  • Shah Jahan also built: Taj Mahal (Agra), Jama Masjid (Delhi), Red Fort (Delhi)

Last verified on 18-01-2026

31

Which resolution serves as a guide for all the constitutions of Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Objectives Resolution
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The Objectives Resolution (1949) serves as the guiding principle for all constitutions of Pakistan.
  • It outlines that sovereignty belongs to Allah, and the state will enable Muslims to live according to Islamic principles while respecting minorities.
  • It was passed by the Constituent Assembly of Pakistan on 12 March 1949.

Last verified on 16-03-2026

32

Until which year did Sindh remain a province of the Arab Empire?

Correct Answer: 1014 AD
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Sindh became part of the Arab Empire in 712 AD after the conquest by Muhammad bin Qasim.
  • It remained under Arab rule until 1014 AD, when local dynasties and later the Ghaznavids took control.
  • Capital of Arab Sindh: Al-Mansura (near modern Sukkur).
  • Arab rule duration: About 303 years.

Last verified on 16-03-2026

33

What is an important part of any government?

Correct Answer: Parliament
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Parliament is an essential part of any government because it makes laws, debates national issues, and represents the people.
  • It ensures democratic governance and checks and balances in the political system.
  • The Parliament usually consists of two houses in many countries: Upper House (Senate) and Lower House (National Assembly / House of Representatives).

Last verified on 16-03-2026

34

Deosai National Park is remarkable as it s one of the highest plateaus in the world with an average elevation of 4,114 meters. In which region of Pakistan is it located?

Correct Answer: Gilgit-Baltistan
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The Deosai National Park is located in Gilgit Baltistan (GB).
  • It was established in 1993.
  • 'Deosai' means 'the land of giants'.
  • It is the second-highest plateau in the world.
  • It has an average elevation of 4,114 meters (13,497 ft) above sea level.

Last verified on 16-03-2026

35

What do the green and white colors of the Pakistan flag represent?

Correct Answer: Both A and B
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Dark green field: Represents Muslim majority in Pakistan.
    • White stripe: Represents religious minorities.
    • Crescent: Symbolizes progress.
    • Star: Symbolizes light and knowledge.

    Colors Meaning:

    • Green: Hope, joy, love, and Islam
    • White: Peace and honesty

    Last verified on 14-03-2026

    36

    Sukkur Multan motorway is known as _____?

    Correct Answer: M5
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The Sukkur-Multan Motorway is known as M5.
    • It is 392 km long.
    • It running from Multan in the north to Sukkur in the south.
    • Major cities along the M5 include: Jalalpur Pirwala, Shujaabād, and Pano Aqil.

    Additional information:

    The Sukkur Barrage, officially named Lloyd Barrage, is located in Sindh Province.

    It built from 1923 to 1932 during the British Raj.

    It was rehabilitated in November 2004. 

    The barrage is approximately 2 km (1 mi) long.

    It is the oldest in Pakistan, with seven canals taking off from it.

    Seven canals take off from Sukkur Barrage:

    Left Bank Canals:

    • 1. Khairpur West Canal
    • 2. Rohri Canal
    • 3. Khairpur East Canal
    • 4. Eastern Nara Canal
    Right Bank Canals:
    • 1. North West Canal
    • 2. Rice Canal
    • 3. Dadu Canal

    Last verified on 06-03-2026

    37

    Who conquered Sindh in 1026?

    Correct Answer: Sultan Mehmood Ghaznavi
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Timeline of Sindh Conquests / Rule

    • 1026 AD: Sindh conquered by Sultan Mehmood Ghaznavi
    • 1050 AD: Soomras take control from Ghaznavides
    • 1351 AD: Samas take control from Soomras
    • 1520–1566 AD: Rule of Sultan Suleiman the Magnificent
    • 1520–1591 AD: Sindh ruled by Shah Baig Argoon, Shah Hassan Argoon, Mirza Isa Tarkhan, etc.
    • 1591 AD: Sindh conquered by Mughals
    • 1701 AD: Kalhoras take control from Mughals
    • 1783 AD: Talpurs overthrow Kalhoras
    • 17 Feb 1843: Sindh annexed by British from Talpurs

    Last verified on 16-05-2026

    38

    Manora Fort (Karachi) was constructed during the rule of _____?

    Correct Answer: Talpur
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Manora Fort was constructed in 1797 during the rule of the Talpur dynasty in Sindh.
    • It was built to protect the Karachi harbor and coastal trade routes.

    Additional information:

    • Ruling Dynasty: Talpur (1783–1843)
    • Location: Manora Island, Karachi
    • Major Trade Links: Oman, Bahrain, Persian Gulf

    Last verified on 16-03-2026

    39

    Main goal of British colonial rule in Punjab?

    Correct Answer: Exploitation of resources
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The main goal of British colonial rule in Punjab was the Exploitation of resources. 

    After annexing Punjab in 1849, the British focused on maximizing revenue through land taxation and transforming the province into a "model agricultural province" to serve British interests.


    40

    The 4-day conflict between Pakistan and India in May 2025 started on?

    Correct Answer: 7 May 2025
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The 4-day military conflict between India and Pakistan from May 7 to May 10, 2025,
    • known as Operation Sindoor
    • In response to a 22 April attack in Pahalgam, Jammu and Kashmir.

    Who was the powerful Sikh ruler of Punjab before 1849?

    Correct Answer: Ranjit Singh
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Maharaja Ranjit Singh was 1st Maharaja of Sikh Empire

    Who founded the Sikh Empire and ruled Punjab from 1801 to 1839

    He is known as the "Lion of Punjab" or Sher-e-Punjab



    42

    Azakhel dry port has recently been inaugurated in which city?

    Correct Answer: Nowshera
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    •  The Azakhel Dry Port was inaugurated in Nowshera, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
    • By former Prime Minister Imran Khan in January 2020
    • Its original name (with diacritics) is Äza Khel Payan


    43

    Who was the founder of the Unionist Party?

    Correct Answer: Sir Fazal Hussain
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The National Unionist Party was a political party based in the Punjab Province

    It was founded by Sir Fazli Husain, Sir Chotu Ram and Sir Sikandar Hayat Khanin 1923

    This party was enforced during the period of British rule in India.

    In the 1937 Indian provincial elections, the Unionist Party soundly defeated the Muslim League in Punjab.

    Unionist Party won 98 seats (out of 175 total), including 78 of the 89 Muslim seats,

    while the Muslim League won only two

    members of the party

    • Sir Sikandar Hayat Khan
    • Sir Khizar Hayat Tiwana
    • Sir Fazli Husain
    • Sir Shahab-ud-Din
    •  Muhammad Hussain Shah
    • Sir Chhotu Ram
    44

    What does Article 175(1) of the Constitution of Pakistan state?

    Correct Answer: Independence of Judiciary
    Show explanation
    Explanation

     175A. (1) There shall be a Judicial Commission of Pakistan, hereinafter in this Article referred to as the Commission, for the appointment of Judges of the

    45

    Begum Naseem Wali Khan, a prominent Pakistani politician and leader of the Awami National Party (ANP), passed away on?

    Correct Answer: 16 May 2021
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • She was one of the main leaders of the Pakistan National Alliance
    • Known for: Provincial Leader of the Awami National Party
    • Born: 4 January 1932
    • Died: May 16, 2021 (aged 89), Charsadda, Pakistan.
    46

    PERA stands for?

    Correct Answer: Punjab Enforcement and Regulatory Authority
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • PERA stands for Punjab Enforcement and Regulatory Authority.
    • To regulate, enforce, and monitor government policies in Punjab.
    • Established in: Punjab, Pakistan.
    • Formed 17 October 2024
    • Motto: Welfare of the Citizens
    • Headquarters: Lahore


    Last updated 15-03-2026

    47

    Which commission divided Punjab into two parts in 1947?

    Correct Answer: Punjab Boundary Commission,
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The Punjab was divided into two parts in 1947 by the Punjab Boundary Commission,
    • Chaired by British lawyer Sir Cyril Radcliffe
    • Four representatives—two from the Muslim League and two from the Indian National Congress (representing Sikhs and Hindus).
    • Outcome: West Punjab (mostly Muslim) went to Pakistan, while East Punjab (mostly Hindu/Sikh) remained in India
    48

    Which group occupied Punjab in 1758?

    Correct Answer: Maratha Empire
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • In 1758, the Maratha Empire occupied Punjab
    • Fought between the Afghan Empire under Ahmad Shah Durrani and the Maratha Empire and the Sikh Confederacy
    • Between 1758 and 1761.
    • Location: Punjab and the region around Delhi
    • Result: Afghan victory
    49

    How many years the 1st Constituent Assembly lasted?

    Correct Answer: 7
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The first Constituent Assembly of Pakistan lasted for approximately seven years
    • Establishment: August 1947
    • Dissolution: 24 October 1954

    Last updated 15-03-2026

    50

    The highest civil award of Pakistan is _____?

    Correct Answer: Nishan-e-Pakistan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

      • Nishan-e-Pakistan – Largest / highest civil award
      • Hilal-i-Imtiaz – Second highest civil award
      • Sitara-e-Imtiaz – Third highest civil award
      • Tamgha-e-Khidmat – Smallest civil award

      Additional information:

      • Nishan-e-Haider – Highest military honor in Pakistan
      • First recipient: Captain Raja Muhammad Sarwar

      Last verified on 15-03-2026

      51

      Pakistan Television (PTV) started its live transmission?

      Correct Answer: November 26, 1964
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      On November 26, 1964, the first official television station in Pakistan commenced broadcasts from Lahore.

      • This was followed by 2nd Dhaka in 1965.
      • 3rd Rawalpindi-Islamabad in 1965.
      • 4th Karachi in 1966.

      Last verified on 08-03-2026

      52

      Which province is known as the "Land of Five Rivers" (Punjab)?

      Correct Answer: Punjab
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Punjab, Pakistan is called the "Land of Five Rivers".
      • Because five major rivers—Jhelum, Chenab, Ravi, Beas, Sutlej—flow through it.

      Additional information:

      There are a total of 24 rivers flowing through Pakistan.

      • 8 rivers flow in KPK
      • 5 in Punjab
      • 4 in Sindh
      • 7 in Balochistan

      Last verified on 15-03-2026

      53

      Which is the highest Military Award of Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Nishan-e-Haider
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • The highest military award is Nishan-e-Haider.
      • Captain Raja Muhammad Sarwar, the country's first recipient of Nishan-i-Haider.
      • Nishan-e-Pakistan: Highest Civil Award.
      • Tamgha-e-Khidmat: Smallest Civil Award.


      Additional information:

      Highest Military Awards in the World List
      Country NameAward Name
      United KingdomThe Victoria Cross
      RussiaOrder of St. George
      ChinaOrder of August First
      United StatesMedal of Honor
      IndiaParam Vir Chakra
      PakistanNishan-e-Haider
      FranceThe Legion of Honour
      GermanyThe Cross of Honour for Valour
      ItalyGold Medal of Military Valour
      JapanOrder of the Chrysanthemum
      GreeceCross of Valour
      TurkeyMedal of Honor
      IsraelMedal of Valor
      AustraliaVictoria Cross for Australia
      BangladeshBir Sreshtho
      CanadaThe Victoria Cross of Canada
      DenmarkThe Valour Cross
      FinlandMannerheim Cross
      North KoreaSoldier’s Medal of Honou
      PolandOrder of Virtuti Militari
      ThailandOrder of Rama
      IranOrder of Zolfaghar
      AfghanistanCampaign Medal
      South AfricaNkwe ya Gauta –
      Golden Leopard

      AustriaMilitary Merit Decoration

      SpainLaureate Cross

      54

      Which is the largest lake in Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Manchar Lake
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Manchar Lake is the largest natural lake in Pakistan.
      • It is part of the Indus River basin.
      • Covered Area: ~260 km² (100 square miles)
      • Location: Jamshoro District, Sindh
      • Water Source: Kirthar Mountains and surrounding streams

      Last verified on 14-03-2026

      55

      The official/old name of Sukkur barrage is?

      Correct Answer: Lloyd Barrage
      Show explanation
      Explanation

        • The Sukkur Barrage, officially named Lloyd Barrage, is located in Sindh Province.
        • It built from 1923 to 1932 during the British Raj.
        • It was rehabilitated in November 2004. 
        • The barrage is approximately 2 km (1 mi) long.
        • It is the oldest in Pakistan, with seven canals taking off from it.

        Last verified on 14-03-2026

        56

        How many seats of Senate (Upper House) are there in Pakistan?

        Correct Answer: 96
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • Current Members of Senate: 96 Senators
        • Previous Strength: 100 members (2021)
        • Earlier Strength: 104 members (after 18th Amendment in 2012)
        • The reduction in seats occurred after the merger of FATA into Khyber Pakhtunkhwa.
        • Term of Members: 6 years
        • Retirement System: ½ members retire every 3 years

        57

        Who becomes Acting President in the absence of President of Pakistan?

        Correct Answer: Chairman Senate
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • According to Article 49(1) of the Constitution of Pakistan, the Chairman Senate acts as President in the President’s absence.
        • Speaker National Assembly as acting Prime Minister of Pakistan in the absence of PM.
        • The term for members of the Senate is 6 years.
        • The minimum age for senators is 30 years.

        Last verified on 14-03-2026

        58

        Whom did Mohen‑jo‑Daro trade with?

        Correct Answer: Iran and Afghanistan
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • Mohenjo-Daro was a major city of the Indus Valley Civilization (2600–1900 BC).
        • Its economy depended heavily on trade and commerce.
        • Archaeological evidence shows trade with Iran and Afghanistan, and even with regions like Mesopotamia.
        • Goods such as cotton, beads, and metals were exchanged through land and sea routes.

        Last verified on 14-03-2026

        59

        Who was the British army leader who led the attack on Miani, Sindh?

        Correct Answer: Charles Napier
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • The Battle of Miani was fought on 17 February 1843.
        • It was between the British East India Company and the Talpur rulers of Sindh.
        • Sir Charles Napier led the British army and defeated the Talpurs.
        • It leading to the annexation of Sindh.

        Last verified on 14-03-2026

        60

        The Dark green field in the flag of Pakistan represents _______?

        Correct Answer: Muslim Majority
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • Dark green field: Represents Muslim majority in Pakistan.
          • White stripe: Represents religious minorities.
          • Crescent: Symbolizes progress.
          • Star: Symbolizes light and knowledge.

          Colors Meaning:

          • Green: Hope, joy, love, and Islam
          • White: Peace and honesty

          The Quaid-e-Azam passed his Bar-at-law examination from ______?

          Correct Answer: Lincoln’s Inn
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah passed his Bar-at-Law examination on 25 May 1893.
          • He studied law at Lincoln’s Inn in London, one of the four Inns of Court.
          • After completing his legal training, he returned to India in 1896 and started his legal career in Bombay.

          • National Bird: Chukar
          • National Animal: Markhor
          • National Dish: Nihari
          • National Fruit: Mango
          • National Tree: Deodar

          67

          When Pakistan came into being, at that time Pakistan had_____provinces.

          Correct Answer: 5
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          When Pakistan was founded in 1947, it consisted of five provinces:

          1. East Bengal (now Bangladesh)
          2. West Punjab (now Punjab)
          3. Sindh
          4. North-West Frontier Province (now Khyber Pakhtunkhwa)
          5. Baluchistan (now Balochistan)

          Additional information:

          • One Unit Policy introduced in 1955 to merge western provinces of Pakistan.
          • Western provinces formed a single unit called West Pakistan.
          • Provincial capital of West Pakistan: Lahore.
          • East Bengal renamed East Pakistan at the same time.
          • Provincial capital of East Pakistan: Dhaka.

          Last verified on 12-03-2026

          68

          Aligarh education Movement was started by Sir Ahmed Khan _____?

          Correct Answer: After the war of independence
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • The Aligarh Educational Movement was started by Sir Syed Ahmad Khan after the War of Independence (1857).

          Additional information:

          • Scientific Society founded: 1864
          • Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental College established: 1875
          • Aligarh Muslim University status granted: 1920

          Last verified on 12-03-2026

          69

          The One-Unit Scheme was introduced in Pakistan to ______?

          Correct Answer: Merge all four provinces into one unit
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • One-Unit Scheme Introduced by: Governor-General Iskander Mirza
          • Year: 1955
          • West Pakistan provinces merged: Punjab, Sindh, NWFP, Baluchistan
          • East Pakistan remained: East Pakistan province
          • Abolished: 1 July 1970

          Last verified on 07-02-2026

          70

          K-2 is the second highest mountain in the world is located in _____?

          Correct Answer: Karakoram Mountain Range
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • K2 is part of the Karakoram Range.
          • It is located on the border between Baltistan, in the Gilgit–Baltistan region of Pakistan.

          Additional information:

          • K-2 is also known as Godwin Austin.
          • 'K' standing for Karakoram.
          • Mount K2 is the second-highest mountain on Earth.
          • Mount K2 is located on the border of Pakistan and China.
          • The height of K2 is 8,611 meters.

          Last verified on 12-03-2026

          71

          Katpana desert is located in ____?

          Correct Answer: Skardu
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Katpana Desert is a cold desert located in Skardu, Gilgit-Baltistan, Pakistan.
          • Popular for photography, jeep safaris, and desert snow landscapes.

          Additional information:

          • Altitude: 2,226 meters above sea level
          • Global ranking: 16th largest desert in the world
          • Approximate area: ~50 km²
          • Average winter temperature: -10°C to 5°C

          Last verified on 12-03-2026

          72

          Lawari pass connects ________?

          Correct Answer: Dir with chitral
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • The Lowari Pass is a high mountain pass within the Khyber-Pakhtunkhwa
          • Location: Connects Chitral and Dir (Lower/Upper).
          • Elevation: 3,118 m (10,230 ft)
          • Range: Hindukush Mountains


          Last updated 11-02-2026

          73

          Lowari Pass connects Peshawar with?

          Correct Answer: Chitral
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The Lowari Pass (or Lowari Top) connects Peshawar (and Dir) with Chitral in the Khyber-Pakhtunkhwa 


          Additional information
          Important Passes of Pakistan

          1. Khyber Pass 

          connects: Peshawar to Kabul

          connects: Pakistan to Afghanistan

          Height 1070 meters, 3510 feet 


          2. Bolan Pass 

          Connects: JCB and Sibi with Quetta 

          Height: 1793 meters, 5884 feet 


          3. Lowari Pass 

          Connects: Chitral with Dir 

          Height: 3118 meters, 10230 feet 


          4. Broghil Pass 

          Location: Pakistan & Afghanistan border 

          Height: 3798 meters, 12460 feet 


          5. Malakand Pass 

          Connects: Dir with Chitral 

          Height: 1362 meters, 4468 feet 


          6. Shandur pass

          Connects: Gupis/Gilgit with Chitral 

          Height: 3700 meters, 12139 feet 


          7. Khanjerab Pass (top most in Pakistan) 

          Other name: Friendship Gate

          Connects: China with Pakistan 

          Height: 4693 meters, 15397 feet 


          8. Dorah pass

          Connects: Badakhshan province of Afghanistan with Chitral 

          Height: 4300 meters, 14108 feet 


          9. Babusar Pass 

          Connects: Mansehra/Abbotabad with Gilgit


          10. Kohat Pass

          Connects: Kohat with Peshawar 


          11. Khojak Pass

          Connects: Qilla Abdullah with Chaman


          12. Tochi Pass

          Connects: Ghazni with Bannu


          13. Karakar Pass

          Connects: Mingora with Buner 



          Last updated 11-02-2026


          74

          Imran Khan was removed from the prime minster office through which method?

          Correct Answer: No-vote of confidence
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Imran Khan was removed through a no-confidence vote in the National Assembly of Pakistan.

          Additional information:

          • Date of removal: 10 April 2022
          • Successor as Prime Minister: Shehbaz Sharif
          • Vote count: 174 votes in favor of no-confidence
          • This was under Article 5 and Article 224 of Pakistan’s Constitution.

          Last verified on 11-03-2026

          75

          Who was the lord Curzon in India during the partition of Bengal 1905?

          Correct Answer: Viceroy of India
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Lord Curzon was the Viceroy of India from 1899 to 1905.
          • In 1905, he partitioned Bengal into East Bengal (Muslim majority) and West Bengal (Hindu majority) for administrative purposes.

          Additional information:

          • The Swadeshi Movement (1905) involved boycotting British goods.
          • The partition was reversed in 1911 due to widespread protests.

          Last verified on 11-03-2026

          76

          Who was the chairman of boundary commission during the partition of Subcontinent in 1947?

          Correct Answer: Cyril Radcliff
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Sir Cyril Radcliffe chaired the Boundary Commission and drew the borders between India and Pakistan.
          • The border he established on 17 August 1947 is famously known as the Radcliffe Line.
          • The announcement of the partition and borders was made by Lord Mountbatten in 1947.

          Last verified on 11-03-2026

          77

          In which city is the newly built Pakistan monument located?

          Correct Answer: Islamabad
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • The Pakistan Monument is a national monument and heritage museum located on the western Shakarparian Hills in Islamabad, Pakistan.

          Additional information:

          • Architectural style: Mughal
          • Construction started: May 25, 2004
          • Opened: March 23, 2007
          • Structural engineer: Mushtaq & Bilal

          Name the event which was implemented by the authorities of the British Raj India in 1905.

          Correct Answer: Partition of Bengal
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Year: 1905
          • Implemented by: British Raj authorities
          • Purpose: Administrative efficiency and divide-and-rule policy
          • Resulting Regions: Eastern Bengal & Assam (Muslim majority), Western Bengal (Hindu majority)
          • Protests: Led to Swadeshi Movement and boycotts of British goods

          81

          Sir Syed Ahmed Khan also realized that Muslims were lagging behind in education because of their conservative approach towards ______?

          Correct Answer: English and modern knowledge
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Sir Syed Ahmed Khan observed that Muslims in India were lagging behind in modern education during the British colonial period.
          • He emphasized learning English and modern sciences alongside traditional Islamic education.
          • This insight led to the establishment of Aligarh Muslim University.

          Additional information:

          • Born: 17 October 1817, Delhi
          • Died: 27 March 1898

          Correct Answer: Germany
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Allama Iqbal was a renowned poet, philosopher, and politician.
          • He obtained his PhD in Philosophy from Germany.
          • His thesis was titled “The Development of Metaphysics in Persia.”
          • He is famously known as the “Poet of the East”.

            Additional information:

            • Born: 9 November 1877, Sialkot
            • Father: Sheikh Noor Muhammad
            • Mother: Imam Bibi
            • Died: 21 April 1938, buried at Badshahi Mosque

                Pakistan purchased Gwadar from Muscat (Oman) in which year?

                Correct Answer: 1958
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • Pakistan purchased the Gwadar enclave from Oman on 8 September 1958 
                • The Gwadar Port is situated on the Arabian Sea at Gwadar in the Balochistan
                • Takeover Date: December 8, 1958.
                • Purchase Amount: $3 million (approximately £3 million).
                • Port Opening: 2002

                85

                1973 Constitution of Pakistan was ______ constitution.

                Correct Answer: Third
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                • The 1973 Constitution of Pakistan is the third constitution in the country's history.
                • It was approved by the National Assembly on 10 April 1973.
                • The constitution came into force on 14 August 1973.
                • It was drafted during the government of Zulfikar Ali Bhutto.
                • It established a parliamentary system of government in Pakistan.
                • It has 280 Articles and 7 Schedules.

                Additional information:

                1956 Constitution – First Constitution

                • 234 Articles, 13 Parts, 6 Schedules
                • Declared Pakistan an Islamic Republic.

                1962 Constitution – Second Constitution

                • 250 Articles, 12 Parts, 3 Schedules
                • Introduced a Presidential system.

                Last verified on 10-03-2026

                • Joined Muslim League: 1908 in London, 1922 in Indian subcontinent.

                90

                Currently largest share in Pakistan’s Electricity generation comes from ______?

                Correct Answer: Thermal Energy
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • In Pakistan, the largest share of electricity generation comes from Thermal Energy.
                • Thermal power plants generate electricity using coal, oil, and natural gas.

                Additional information:

                • One of the largest thermal power plants is Kot Addu Power Company (KAPCO) in Kot Addu, Punjab.
                • Capacity: Approximately 1600 MW, making it one of Pakistan’s major electricity-producing plants.

                Last verified on 09-03-2026

                91

                How many times Pakistan became non permanent member of UNO Security Council?

                Correct Answer: 8 times
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • Pakistan has been elected 8 times as a non-permanent member of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC).
                • The UN Security Council has 15 members: 5 permanent and 10 non-permanent members.
                • Non-permanent members are elected for a 2-year term by the UN General Assembly.

                Additional information:

                Pakistan’s Terms in UN Security Council

                1. 1952 – 1953
                2. 1968 – 1969
                3. 1976 – 1977
                4. 1983 – 1984
                5. 1993 – 1994
                6. 2003 – 2004
                7. 2012 – 2013
                8. 2025 – 2026

                Last verified on 09-03-2026

                92

                When Pakistan officially joined United Nations Organisations (UNO)?

                Correct Answer: 30 September 1947
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • Pakistan joined the United Nations on September 30, 1947.
                • The name was coined by US President Franklin D. Roosevelt.
                • Headquarters is located in New York, United States.
                • Founded on October 24, 1945.
                • It currently has 193 member states and 2 observer states.
                • The official languages of the UN are Arabic, Chinese, English, French, Russian, and Spanish.


                It has been a non-permanent member of the UN Security Council seven times in:
                1. 1952-1953
                2. 1968-1969
                3. 1976-1977
                4. 1983-1984
                5. 1993-1994
                6. 2003-2004
                7. 2012-2013


                Last updated on 09-03-2026

                What is the total area of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KPK)?

                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • The total area of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KPK) is approximately 74,521 square kilometers
                • Located in the northwestern region
                • The fourth-largest province of Pakistan by land area
                • The third-largest province by population. 
                • Area: 39,282 mi²
                • Capital and largest city: Peshawar
                • Established (as NWFP): 1 July 1970
                • Renamed: 19 April 2010

                94

                What is the length of Siachen Glacier?

                Correct Answer: 76 KM
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                 NOTE: کچھ ذرائع کے مطابق یہ لمبائی 72.4 کلومیٹر یعنی 45 میل ہے۔ پیپر میں76 یا 72 آئے ان دونوں میں جو بھی آئے اس کے مطابق جواب دیں


                • Siachen Glacier length is 76KM
                • Located in the eastern Karakoram range of the Himalayas,
                • Located in Ghanche District, Gilgit-Baltistan.
                • Height  20,000 feet
                •  Pakistan's largest Glacier
                • World 2nd largest Glacier
                • The  meaning of Siachen Glacier is Abundance of roses.گلاب کی کثرت۔

                95

                Which river flows in Balochistan? Zhob, Dasht, Gomal, All of these

                Correct Answer: All of these
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                All of these rivers (Zhob, Dasht, and Gomal) flow in or border the province of Balochistan


                There are many rivers in Balochistan

                The important rivers in Balochistan are:

                1. Zhob
                2. Nari
                3. Bolan
                4. Pishin
                5. Lora
                6. Mula
                7. Hub
                8. Porali
                9. Hingol is the largest river in Balochistan
                10. Rakshan
                11. Dasht.

                ***

                while

                24 rivers are present in Pakistan

                5 rivers flow in Punjab Province

                4 rivers flow in Sindh Province

                7 rivers flow in Balochistan Province

                8 rivers flow in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Province

                Last updated on 09-03-2026


                96

                Where is Baltoro Glacier located?

                Correct Answer: Gilgit Baltistan
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • The Baltoro Glacier is located in the Shigar District of Gilgit-Baltistan
                • Surrounded by K2
                • Location: Karakoram range, Gilgit-Baltistan
                • Area: 1,500 square kilometres (580 sq mi)
                • Length: 63 kilometres (39 mi)


                Last updated on 09-03-2026

                97

                Which is the short range ballistic missile of Pakistan?

                Correct Answer: Ghaznavi
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • The Ghaznavi Hatf–III is a land-based short-range ballistic missile
                • Ghaznavi — (290–320 km)
                • In service: 2004–Present
                • Designer: National Defence Complex
                • Designed: 1994–98


                Additional information 

                Shaheen-I — (750–1000 km)  (SRBM)

                Abdali — (180–450 km )  (BRBM)

                Ghauri-I — (1,500 km)[9]  (MRBM)

                Ghauri-II — (2,000–2,300 km)  (MRBM)



                Last updated on 09-03-2026

                98

                Which country has developed the Abdali Short Range Ballistic Missiles (SRBMs)?

                Correct Answer: Pakistan
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • The Abdali Short Range Ballistic Missile (SRBM) was developed by Pakistan.
                • Also known as Hatf-II
                • Developed by Pakistan’s Space Research Commission (SUPARCO).
                •  It entered service in 2005 as a tactical weapon
                • Type: Short-range, solid-propellant, surface-to-surface ballistic missile
                • Role: Tactical, road-mobile (transporter-erector-launcher)
                • Range: Approximately 450km


                 ***

                Last updated on 09-03-2026

                99

                The largest river of Pakistan is located in?

                Correct Answer: Sindh
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • The National and largest river of Pakistan is the Indus River. 
                • Located in Sindh
                • Also known as the Sindhu River
                • Starts making a delta near the city of Thatta
                • Originates from the Mansarovar lake
                • Flows through India, Pakistan,
                • Finally falls into the Arabian Sea near Karachi.
                • Old name of the Indus River was Sindhu
                • 6 Barrages constructed
                • Length: 3,180 km
                • 2nd source: Gar Tsangpo
                • The upper Indus plain ends at Mithankot 
                • The upper Indus plain starts from Kalabagh

                Last updated on 09-03-2026

                100

                Torkham Border connects Pakistan with which country?

                Correct Answer: Afghanistan
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • The Torkham border connects Pakistan with Afghanistan
                • It connects Nangarhar province (Afghanistan) with Pakistan's KPK province
                • It is the busiest port of entry
                • Length:  2,600km-long porous border 
                • linking Pakistan’s N-5 National Highway


                Last updated on 09-03-2026

                101

                Sukkur Barrage located on which river?

                Correct Answer: Indus River
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • The Sukkur Barrage is located on the Indus River
                • Formerly known as Lloyd Barrage.
                • Built during the British Raj from 1923 to 1932 
                • Located in Sindh Province
                • Latest built in November 2004
                • Completed in 1932
                • Length: Approximately 2 km (1 mi)
                • The oldest barrage in Pakistan.
                • 7 canals take off from the Sukkur Barrage


                Last updated on 09-03-2026

                102

                Who chose the name of Pakistan?

                Correct Answer: Chaudry Rehmat Ali
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • The name "Pakistan" was coined by Chaudhry Rahmat Ali, 
                • He is a prominent activist of the Pakistan Movement.
                • He first published it in a 1933 pamphlet titled "Now or Never".
                • The name translates to "Land of the Pure" in Urdu 
                • He first thought of the name in 1932 while on a London bus,
                • it was formally published in January 1933. 


                  Rahmat Ali created it as an acronym for
                • Punjab
                • Afghania (North-West Frontier Province)
                • Kashmir
                • Sindh
                • Baluchistan 
                Question updated on 08 march 2026

                103

                Who is the Current School Education Minister of Punjab?

                Correct Answer: Rana Sikandar Hayat
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Rana Sikandar Hayat is the current Minister for School Education of Punjab.

                Belonging: Kasur

                Party affiliation: Pakistan Muslim League (Nawaz)

                Appointed on 6th March 2024

                 

                Last updated on 04-03-2026

                104

                The war of 1857 had ended in disaster however the glorious Mughal Empire was ended. Name the type of British rule established over the whole sub-continent?

                Correct Answer: Direct rule
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • After the Indian Rebellion of 1857, the British East India Company was dissolved.
                • The British Crown took direct control over India, marking the start of the British Raj (1858–1947).
                • The Mughal Emperor was removed, ending centuries of Mughal rule.

                Last verified on 08-03-2026

                105

                Which side of the Indus Water gave control in Pakistan as per the Indus Water Treaty of 1960?

                Correct Answer: Western tributaries
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • The Indus Water Treaty (1960) was signed between Pakistan and India to share the Indus River system.
                  • Pakistan got control of the western rivers: Indus, Jhelum, and Chenab.
                  • India got control of the eastern rivers: Ravi, Beas, and Sutlej.

                  Last verified on 08-03-2026

                  106

                  What Muhammad Ali Jinnah not wrote in his famous 14 points for the Muslim of India?

                  Correct Answer: Joint electorate
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • Muhammad Ali Jinnah’s 14 Points (1929) were guidelines to protect Muslim political and religious rights in India.
                    • They proposed proportional representation, provincial autonomy, separate electorates, and full religious freedom.
                    • Joint electorate was not included; he demanded separate electorates for Muslims to safeguard their political voice.

                    Last verified on 08-03-2026

                    107

                    The Lower House of Pakistan is Called?

                    Correct Answer: National Assembly of Pakistan
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • National Assembly Founded in 1947, Karachi.
                    • National Assembly is the Lower House of Parliament.
                    • Senate is the Upper house of Parliament.
                    • Old member 342 now 336.

                    NamePunjabSindhKPKBaluchistanICTTotal
                    General1416145163266
                    Women3214104-60
                    Non-Muslims-----10
                    Total1737555203336


                    Last verified on 08-03-2026

                    108

                    Hadero lake is located in _______?

                    Correct Answer: Thatta District, Sindh
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • Hadero Lake is a natural lake located in Thatta District of Sindh, Pakistan.
                    • It lies near the Indus Delta region.
                    • It is an important wetland habitat.
                    • The lake supports migratory birds, fish, and wildlife.

                    Additional information:

                    • Manchar Lake – Jamshoro/Dadu (largest lake of Pakistan)
                    • Keenjhar Lake – Thatta (major drinking water source for Karachi)
                    • Haleji Lake – Thatta (Ramsar wetland)
                    • Chotiari Reservoir – Sanghar District

                    Last verified on 08-03-2026

                    109

                    Pakistan’s main export item is ______?

                    Correct Answer: Cotton and cotton manufactures
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • Pakistan’s economy largely depends on the textile sector, which is based on cotton production.
                    • Cotton and cotton manufactured products are the largest share of Pakistan’s exports.
                    • The textile industry includes yarn, cloth, garments, and towels exported worldwide.
                    • Major export markets include USA, China, UK, Germany, and UAE.

                    Last verified on 08-03-2026

                    110

                    Worldwide Nanga Parbat is _______ highest mountain.

                    Correct Answer: 9
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    Worldwide Nanga Parbat is the 9th highest mountain.

                    Additional information:

                    Rk.MountainHeight(M)Height(F)RangeParent mountainFirst ascentCountry

                    5Makalu8,48527,838Mahalangur HimalayaMount Everest1955Nepal, China
                    6Cho Oyu8,18826,864Mahalangur HimalayaMount Everest1954Nepal, China

                    Last verified on 08-03-2026

                    111

                    Total length of Makran coastal highway that connects Karachi and Gawadar is _______ Km?

                    Correct Answer: 653
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • The Makran Coastal Highway (N10) spans 653 km (406 mi).
                    • It connecting Karachi to Gwadar.
                    • Construction began in 2002.
                    • It was completed in 2004, with the highway operational since 2004.

                    Last verified on 08-03-2026

                    112

                    Pakistan is a member of all following International Organizations except? SAARC, UNO, SCO, G20

                    Correct Answer: G20
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    Pakistan is not a member of the G20, which consists of the world's largest economies.
                    • UNO – Pakistan joined 30 September 1947
                    • SAARC – Pakistan became a founding member in 1985
                    • SCO – Pakistan became a full member in 2017
                    Last verified on 08-03-2026
                    113

                    The Pakistan Navy's primary flag is called the?

                    Correct Answer: Naval Jack
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • The Naval Jack is the primary flag of the Pakistan Navy
                    • It is a blue flag featuring the naval crest in white
                    • Adopted in 1960 to replace the previous green jack.
                    • The design consists of a star and crescent placed above an anchor, all in white on a blue field, with a 2:3 proportion


                    Last updated on 08-March-2026

                    114

                    Who did imposed third Martial Law in Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: Gen. Muhammad Zia ul Haq
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • Army Chief, General Muhammad Zia ul Haq Imposed Third Martial Law in Pakistan on 05-07-1977.

                    Additional information:

                    • First Martial Law: 7th October 1958 by Iskandar Mirza
                    • 2nd Martial Law: 25th March 1969 by General Yahya Khan
                    • 4th Martial Law: 12th October 1999 by General Pervaiz Musharraf

                    Last verified on 07-03-2026

                    115

                    Who introduced the Basic Democracy?

                    Correct Answer: Ayoub Khan
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • After the Martial Law of 1958, General Muhammad Ayub Khan introduced the Basic Democracy System in 1959.
                      • It created a local government structure and an indirect electoral system in Pakistan.

                    Additional information:

                    • Total Basic Democrats: 80,000
                    • East Pakistan: 40,000
                    • West Pakistan: 40,000
                    • These 80,000 members formed the Electoral College to elect the President and Assemblies.

                    Last verified on 07-03-2026

                    116

                    Which city is known as city of Saints?

                    Correct Answer: Multan
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • Multan is famously called the “City of Saints”
                    • Due to its many Sufi shrines and mystics.

                    Additional information:

                    • Province: Punjab, Pakistan
                    • Located on: Chenab River
                    • Population Rank: 7th largest city in Pakistan
                    • Union Councils: 4

                    Last updated by NA on 23-April-2026

                    117

                    The largest producer of dates in Pakistan is?

                    Correct Answer: Khairpur
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • Khairpur District in Sindh is the largest producer of dates in Pakistan.

                    Additional information:

                    • Date Varieties: Aseel, Dhakki, Mehran, Begum Jangi
                    • Annual Production: Around 150,000–200,000 tons (approx.)

                    Last verified on 07-03-2026

                    118

                    The biggest/largest district of Pakistan by area?

                    Correct Answer: Chaghi
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    • Chaghi District in Balochistan is the largest district of Pakistan by area.
                    • It covers over 38,000 km².

                    Additional information:

                    • Lahore District in Punjab is the largest district of Pakistan by population.
                    • According to the 2017 Census, it had a population of 11,126,285.

                    Last verified on 07-03-2026

                    119

                    Motorway M2 is between which two of the following cities?

                    Correct Answer: Lahore to Islamabad
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • The M-2 Motorway connects Lahore and Islamabad.

                    Additional information:

                    • Length: 375 km
                    • Constructed: 1992
                    • North End: Kashmir Highway Interchange, Islamabad
                    • South End: Thokar Niaz Baig, Lahore

                    Last verified on 07-03-2026

                    120

                    Pakistan won the Olympics gold medal for the last time in which game?

                    Correct Answer: Javelin Throw
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                      • Pakistan won an Olympic gold medal most recently at the 2024 Paris Olympics
                      • Most Recent Gold: 2024 (Arshad Nadeem, Javelin Throw)
                      • Last Hockey Gold: 1984 (Los Angeles)
                      • Previous Hockey Golds: 1968 (Mexico City), 1960 (Rome) 
                      • Total Official Medals: 10
                      • Gold Medals: 3 (all in Men’s Field Hockey)
                      • Pakistan won gold in hockey in 1960, 1968, and 1984.

                      Last verified on 07-03-2026

                      121

                      Kartarpur Corridor is in the district of _____?

                      Correct Answer: Narowal
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      • Kartarpur Corridor is a visa-free border corridor between Pakistan and India for Sikh pilgrims.
                      • It is located in Narowal District, Punjab, Pakistan.
                      • The corridor allows Indian Sikhs to visit Gurdwara Darbar Sahib in Kartarpur.
                      • Opened: 9 November 2019
                      • Length: 4.7 km

                      Last verified on 07-03-2026

                      122

                      The first rocket launched by Pakistan was ______?

                      Correct Answer: Rehbar
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      • The first rocket launched by Pakistan was Rahbar-1 (also spelled Rehbar-1).
                      • Rehbar-I, a two-stage solid fuel rocket,
                      • It was launched on June 7, 1962
                      • Launched by SUPARCO 
                      • SUPARCO stands for Space and Upper Atmosphere Research Commission.

                      Last updated on 06-March-2026

                      123

                      Karachi to Islamabad direction?

                      Correct Answer: South to north
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      • Karachi to Islamabad is a move from the south to the north 
                      • Islamabad is located near the eastern side of Karachi.
                      • The distance between Karachi and Islamabad is 1145 km. 
                      •  Karachi to Islamabad, Connected by Multan Sukkur Motorway 

                      Last updated on 06-March-2026



                      124

                      What is the height of Tarbela Dam

                      Correct Answer: 143 m
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      • Tarbela Dam is 143 metres (470 ft) high above the riverbed.
                      • It is the largest earth-filled dam in the world
                      • Max Elevation: 1,550 feet (472.44  m).
                      • Length: 9,000 feet (2743 m).
                      • Capacity: 11.6 million acre-feet (MAF)
                      • Location: Haripur District, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa,
                      • Construction began: 1968
                      • Opening date: 1976

                      Last updated on 06-March-2026

                      125

                      Pak China Boundary line was demarcated during the era of _______?

                      Correct Answer: Ayyub Khan
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      • The Pak-China boundary line was demarcated during the era of President Ayub Khan.
                      • Signed on 2 March 1963.
                      • Signed by Zulfikar Ali Bhutto (Pakistan) and Chen Yi (China)
                      • The Pak-China agreement is called the Sino-Pak border 

                      Last updated on 06-March-2026

                      126

                      The Friendship Gate is built on the border between Pakistan and _____?

                      Correct Answer: Afghanistan
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      • The Friendship Gate is built on the border between Pakistan and Afghanistan.
                      • Located at the Chaman border in Balochistan,
                      • The Spin Boldak District in Kandahar, Afghanistan,
                      • This crossing is known as the Afghanistan–Pakistan Friendship Gate. 
                      • The gate was erected in 2003
                      • On 1 December 2003, the gate was opened

                      Last updated on 06-March-2026

                      127

                      Who was the founder of Islamic State in India?

                      Correct Answer: Shahabuddin Ghori
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      • Shahabuddin Ghori is the founder of Muslim rule in India
                      • Born in 1162.
                      • He was a warrior of Turkish origin 
                      • He traveled from Ghor, Afghanistan, and conquered Delhi in 1192. 
                      • Conquests: He captured Multan and Uch in 1175, and expanded into Lahore in 1186.


                      Question updated 03-March-2026

                      130

                      Which is the capital of Azad Jammu and Kashmir?

                      Correct Answer: Muzaffarabad
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      • Muzaffarabad is the capital and largest city of Azad Jammu and Kashmir
                      • Established 1646
                      • Founded by Sultan Muzaffar Khan
                      • Situated at the Jhelum and Neelum rivers

                      Additional information
                      AJK was established on October 24, 1947.
                      The region covers an area of 13,297 km² (5,134 sq mi).
                      Last updated on 06-March-2026


                      131

                      Murree is located in ?

                      Correct Answer: Pir Panjal Range
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      • Lying in the Galyat region of the Pir Panjal Range
                      • Under the western Himalayas,
                      • It is located in the northeast of the capital city of Islamabad
                      • Murree was constructed in 1853 
                      • Murree was the summer headquarters of colonial Punjab
                      • It has an average altitude of 2,291 metres (7,516 ft).


                      Last updated on 06-March-2026

                      132

                      Dr. Abdul Qadeer Khan obtained his physics degree from___?

                      Correct Answer: Karachi
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      • Dr. Abdul Qadeer Khan obtained a Bachelor's (Physics): University of Karachi (1956)
                      • Master's/Engineer's Degree: Delft University of Technology
                      • PhD (Metallurgical Engineering): Katholieke Universiteit Leuven, Belgium
                      • Born on 1st April 1936 in Bhopal, India
                      • Title: Mohsin-e-Pakistan
                      • Died on 10 October 2021
                      • Died at age of 85 with lung problems.
                      • Founded: Tehreek-e-Tahaffuz-e-Pakistan (TTP)

                      Last updated on 06-March-2026

                      133

                      KPK stands for?

                      Correct Answer: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      • KPK stands for Khyber Pakhtunkhwa 
                      • Established in 1 July 1970
                      • Renamed in 19 April 2010
                      • FATA merged on 31 May 2018
                      • The capital and largest city is Peshawar
                      • Total Area 101,741 sq.km,
                      • Located in the northwestern region
                      • 36 districts and 8th divisions.
                      • The fourth-largest province of Pakistan by land area
                      • The third-largest province by population. 


                      Last updated on 06-March-2026

                      134

                      The first constituent assembly of Pakistan was dissolved mainly due to ______?

                      Correct Answer: Governor-General's intervention
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      • The first Constituent Assembly of Pakistan (1947–1954) was tasked with framing the constitution.
                      • It was dissolved by Governor-General Ghulam Muhammad on 24 October 1954.

                      Additional information:

                      • Assembly formed: 10 August 1947
                      • First Prime Minister: Liaquat Ali Khan
                      • Constitution eventually passed: 1956

                      Last verified on 07-02-2026

                      135

                      When world war-II ended who convened conferences of Indian, political leaders at Simla on 24th June 1945?

                      Correct Answer: Lord Wavell
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      • After World War II ended in 1945, Lord Wavell, then Viceroy of India, convened the Simla Conference on 24 June 1945.
                        • The purpose was to discuss forming an interim government in India before independence.
                        • Leaders from Congress and Muslim League attended to negotiate power-sharing.

                        Last verified on 06-03-2026

                        136

                        The title “Ambassador of Hindu-Muslim-Unity” was given to Mr Jinnah by ____?

                        Correct Answer: Sarojini-Naidu
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation
                        • Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah was given the title “Ambassador of Hindu-Muslim Unity”.
                        • This title was conferred by Sarojini Naidu, a prominent Indian political leader and poet.
                        • At that time, Jinnah was working for unity between Hindus and Muslims.

                        Last verified on 13-01-2026

                        He belongs to PPP.

                        Which is the world’s most polluted city, as per the US Air Quality Index?

                        Correct Answer: Lahore

                        1. Lahore, Pakistan

                        2. Delhi, India 

                        3. Mexico City, Mexico
