                        139

                        Which of the following barrages was first commissioned to work?

                        Correct Answer: Sukkur Barrage
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation
                        • Sukkur Barrage is the first major barrage built on the Indus River in Pakistan.
                        • It was commissioned in 1932 during British India to control floods and provide irrigation.
                        • It is located near Sukkur, Sindh.
                        • It has 66 gates to control water flow for farming and flood management.

                        Additional information:

                        • Kotri Barrage – 1955
                        • Gudu Barrage – 1968
                        • Chashma Barrage – 1971

                        Last verified on 06-03-2026

                        140

                        The mount Godwin Austen is the name of ______?

                        Correct Answer: K2
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        K2 World 2nd highest mountain

                        • K2 Elevation: 8,611 m
                        • K2 First ascent: July 31, 1954
                        • K2 Mountain range: Karakoram range
                        • K2 First ascenders: Achille Compagnoni, Lino Lacedelli
                        • K2 Easiest route: Abruzzi Spur

                        Last verified on 06-03-2026

                        141

                        Pakistan won the Olympic medal in men hockey contest for the last time in?

                        Correct Answer: 1984 (Los Andles)
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation
                        • First International Match: 2 August 1948, led by Ali Iqtidar Shah Dara vs Belgium, won 2–0 at 1948 London Olympics.
                          • World Hockey Cup Titles (4 times): 1971, 1978, 1982, 1994
                          • 1971 World Cup: Pakistan defeated Spain to become champions.
                          • 1994: Pakistan won the Hockey World Cup for the last time.
                          • 2012 Hockey Champions Trophy: Pakistan beat India to win bronze.

                          Last verified on 06-03-2026

                          142

                          The highest lake of Pakistan is ______?

                          Correct Answer: Rush Lake
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation
                          • Rush Lake is the highest lake in Pakistan.
                          • It is one of the highest alpine lakes in the world.
                          • It is located in Nagar Valley, Gilgit-Baltistan near Rush Peak.
                          • The lake lies at an altitude of about 4,694 meters (15,400 feet) above sea level.
                          • It is a famous destination for trekkers and mountaineers.

                          Last verified on 06-03-2026

                          143

                          The Hindu Kush Range stretches across the territories of _____?

                          Correct Answer: Afghistan and Pakistan
                          Show explanation
                          Explanation
                          • The Hindu Kush Range is a major mountain range in Central and South Asia.
                          • It mainly stretches across Afghanistan and northern Pakistan.
                          • The range forms part of the Himalayan mountain system.
                          • It is about 800 km long and separates Central Asia from the Indian Subcontinent.

                            Additional information:

                            • Himalayas – Located in Pakistan, India, Nepal, Bhutan, China
                              Karakoram Range – Pakistan, China, India
                              Pamir Mountains – Tajikistan, Afghanistan, China
                              Tien Shan – Kyrgyzstan, Kazakhstan, China

                              Last verified on 06-03-2026

                              144

                              Who was the first President of Indian National Congress?

                              Correct Answer: Womesh Chunder Bonnerjee
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • Womesh Chunder Bonnerjee was the first president of the Indian National Congress.
                              • After the party's foundation in 1885
                              • He presided over the inaugural session held in Bombay from December 28 to 31, 1885

                              Additional information

                              • Hewas an Indian barrister
                              • Presided over the 1892 Allahabad session of the Congress.
                              • Born December 29, 1844, Kolkata, India
                              • Died: July 21, 1906, Bedford Park, London, United Kingdom

                              Question updated 03-March-2026

                              145

                              In which year Urdu Hindi controversy?

                              Correct Answer: 1867
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • The Urdu-Hindi controversy officially began in 1867 in India
                              • It started in the United Provinces of Agra and Oudh
                              • Hindus demanded that Hindi be an official language in place of Urdu
                              • In 1881, Hindi replaced Urdu in the Devanagari script in Bihar,
                              • The demand grew significantly in 1867 in Banaras


                              Question updated 03-March-2026



                              146

                              When Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto became the prime minister of Pakistan?

                              Correct Answer: 1971
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • Zulfikar Ali Bhutto became the Prime Minister of Pakistan on August 14, 1973.
                              • 4th President of Pakistan from December 20, 1971, to August 13, 1973.
                              • 9th Prime Minister of Pakistan from August 14, 1973. to July 5, 1977
                              • Founder of the Pakistan People's Party
                              • Nickname: Quaid-e-Awam ("the People's Leader")
                              • Born on January 5, 1928, in Larkana
                              • Hanged to death on 4 April 1979, District Jail Rawalpindi


                              Question updated 03-March-2026





                              147

                              What is the name of the counter-terrorism operation by the Pakistan Army that killed many terrorists in early March 2026?

                              Correct Answer: Operation Ghazab Lil Haq
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              On 26 February 2026, Afghanistan launched a retaliatory operation against Pakistan. In response, Pakistan launched Operation Ghazab Lil Haq against the Taliban. This operation was initiated following an escalation in border skirmishes and cross-border firing incidents along the Durand Line, involving the Afghan Taliban and Pakistani security forces.The following day, Pakistan announced a state of "open war" with Afghanistan.

                               

                              Additional Information:

                              On 21 February 2026, Pakistan conducted airstrikes in Afghanistan with the aim of targeting militant camps belonging to the Pakistani Taliban (TTP) and the Islamic State – Khorasan Province (ISIS–K) in retaliation for recent terrorist attacks in Islamabad, Bajaur, and Bannu.

                              ****

                              148

                              What is the name of the Pakistani military operation launched against Afghan Taliban forces in 2026?

                              Correct Answer: Operation Ghazab lil-Haq
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              On 26 February 2026, Afghanistan launched a retaliatory operation against Pakistan. In response, Pakistan launched Operation Ghazab Lil Haq against the Taliban. This operation was initiated following an escalation in border skirmishes and cross-border firing incidents along the Durand Line, involving the Afghan Taliban and Pakistani security forces.The following day, Pakistan announced a state of "open war" with Afghanistan.

                               

                              Additional Information:

                              On 21 February 2026, Pakistan conducted airstrikes in Afghanistan with the aim of targeting militant camps belonging to the Pakistani Taliban (TTP) and the Islamic State – Khorasan Province (ISIS–K) in retaliation for recent terrorist attacks in Islamabad, Bajaur, and Bannu.

                              ****

                              149

                              Which province of Pakistan has the longest coastline?

                              Correct Answer: Balochistan
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Balochistan has the longest coastline in Pakistan


                              The coastline of Pakistan is bifurcated into two parts:

                              • Sindh Coast (250 km) 
                              • Makran Coast (770-800 km).

                              The new length of the coastline of Pakistan is 1058 km

                              The old length of the coastline is 1046KM



                              Last updated question 26 feb 2026
                              150

                              The Thar Desert is located in which part of Pakistan?

                              Correct Answer: Eastern
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              The Thar Desert of Sindh is a desert situated in Sindh

                              The southeastern region of Pakistan


                              About Thar Desert

                              • 15% located in Pakistan
                              • 85% located in India
                              • Also called Friendly Desert
                              • Located near Badin, Sindh 
                              • Famous for: Sand 
                              • Coal also found in Thar 
                              • Also known as Great Indian Desert
                              • 7th largest desert on the planet.
                              • 3rd largest in Asia.
                              • Area: 77,000 Miles

                              ****************

                              151

                              “The Millat and Menace of Indianism” is written by _______?

                              Correct Answer: Ch. Rehmat Ali
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • “The Millat and Menace of Indianism” was written by Chaudhry Rehmat Ali.

                              Additional information:

                              • Famous For: Coining the name Pakistan (1933 pamphlet “Now or Never”).
                              • Field: Political thinker and activist of the Pakistan Movement.
                              • Main Idea of Book: Criticized the concept of Indian nationalism and supported a separate Muslim homeland.
                              • Birth–Death: 1897 – 1951.

                              Last verified on 23-02-2026

                              152

                              The word “Bunyan Marsus” is mentioned in which Surah of the Qur’an?

                              Correct Answer: Surah As-Saff
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • Operation Bunyan Um Marsoos was launched by Pakistan on 10 May 2025, after Fajr prayer.
                              • It targeted seven key Indian military sites, including Pathankot, Udhampur, Gujarat air bases, Rajasthan air base, and BrahMos storage site.
                              • The operation was retaliation for Indian missile strikes inside Pakistan.
                              • The name Bunyan Um Marsoos comes from Surah Al-Saff (61:4), meaning “a structure firmly joined together.”
                              • Verse reference: “Allah indeed loves those who fight in His Way as though they are a solid wall cemented with molten lead.”


                              Last verified on 22-02-2026

                              153

                              Nehru report was preparedic in the chairmanship of

                              Correct Answer: Motilal Nehru
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • The Nehru Report was prepared under the chairmanship of Motilal Nehru.
                              • He was a senior leader of the Indian National Congress and led the committee in 1928.
                              • Aimed:  propose a constitutional framework for self-government in India.
                              • Published on August 28, 1928, at the Lucknow conference.


                              Last updated 18-02-2026

                              154

                              Malakhra is a traditional game of _____, Pakistan?

                              Correct Answer: Sindh
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Malakhra is a traditional game of Sindh, Pakistan


                              Origin: It dates back 5,000 years to the Indus Valley Civilization.


                              Competitors, known as malhoo

                              The match begins with both wrestlers (known as malhoos or mulhs)


                              Last updated question  18-02-2026

                              155

                              The oath of the Governor General was administered to Quaid_e_Azam by __________?

                              Correct Answer: Justice Abdur Rasheed
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              The oath of the Governor General was administered to Quaid-e-Azam by Justice Mian Sir Abdur Rashid.


                              He was the first Chief Justice of Pakistan (or Chief Justice of the Lahore High Court )


                              He appointed him on 15 August 1947


                              Jinnah served in this position until his death on September 11, 1948.


                              Last updated question  18-02-2026

                              156

                              Maktubat Imam Rabbani was written by?

                              Correct Answer: Shaykh Ahmad Sirhindi
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Maktubat Imam Rabbani was written by Shaykh Ahmad Sirhindi (also known as Mujaddid Alf Sani).


                              It is a collection of 536 letters  (compiled in three volumes)

                              • Volume 1 (Durr al-Ma'rifat): Compiled by Khwajah Yar Muhammad Badakhshi Talqani, contains 313 letters.
                              • Volume 2 (Noor al-Khallaq): Compiled by Khwajah Abdul Hayy, contains 99 letters.
                              • Volume 3 (Ma'rifat al-Haqa'iq): Compiled by Khwajah Muhammad Hashim Kashmi, contains 124 letters. 

                              Last updated question  18-02-2026

                              157

                              Taxila is the origin of which civilization?

                              Correct Answer: Gandhara Civilization
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              Taxila is intellectual hub of the ancient Gandharan civilization.


                              Old name: Takhshasila


                              Old Taxila was the capital of Gandhara


                              It was designated a UNESCO World Heritage site in 1980


                              Taxila's Province in Punjab


                              Taxila's Division is Rawalpindi Division


                              Last updated question  18-02-2026

                              158

                              Which is NOT a fundamental education body in Pakistan? HEC, FDE, FBISE, UNESCO

                              Correct Answer: UNESCO
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              UNESCO (United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization)


                              It is a specialized UN agency operating in Pakistan, not a domestic governmental body.


                              Additional information:

                              • HEC (Higher Education Commission) 
                              • FDE (Federal Directorate of Education) 
                              • FBISE (Federal Board of Intermediate and Secondary Education) 
                              159

                              Educational policy of Pakistan is called?

                              Correct Answer: NEP (National Education Policy)
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation

                              The overarching strategic framework for education in Pakistan is consistently referred to as the National Education Policy (NEP).


                              Since 1947, the government has introduced several versions of this policy, such as NEP 1998–2010, NEP 2009, and the NEP 2017–2025,

                              161

                              Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto introduced land reforms and reduced the landholding limit to 150 acres for irrigated land and 300 acres for barani land in?

                              Correct Answer: 1972
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                            • Zulfikar Ali Bhutto's first speech on December 20, 1971, focused on introducing reforms and tackling corruption.
                            • The 1972 Land Reforms limited individual holdings to 150 acres of irrigated and 300 acres of un-irrigated land.
                            • Excessive land holdings were taken over without compensation.
                            • Over 800,000 acres of agricultural land were redistributed.
                            • Last verified on 12-02-2026
                              • 162

                              Olericulture is the study dealing with ______?

                              Correct Answer: Vegetable production
                              Show explanation
                              Explanation
                              • Olericulture is a branch of horticulture.
                                • It focuses on the cultivation, growth, and harvesting of vegetables.
                                • Helps improve yield, quality, and pest management in vegetables.
                                • Important for food supply and nutrition.
                                • Examples include tomatoes, carrots, and cabbage.

                                Last verified on 12-02-2026

                                163

                                To decrease water loss during transmission, a mega project for lining of water courses and canals was launched in 2004-05 with a cost of Rs. ______?

                                Correct Answer: 66 billion
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                • In 2004, Pakistan launched the National Program for Improvement of Watercourses (NPIW).
                                • Project cost: Rs. 66 billion (initial estimate).

                                Additional information:

                                • The project covered approximately 86,000 watercourses across Pakistan.
                                • Seepage and conveyance losses account for 40–50% of water loss before reaching crops.
                                • Objective: Minimize water losses, improve irrigation efficiency, and support agriculture.

                                Last verified on 11-02-2026

                                164

                                Of the total forest area of Pakistan, commercial forests cover _____?

                                Correct Answer: 33%
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                • Pakistan’s forests are classified into commercial (production) and protection forests.
                                • Total forest cover is low, estimated between 2% and 5.7% of the country’s area.
                                • Around one-third (33%) of forest area is commercial, managed for timber and other products.

                                Last verified on 11-02-2026

                                165

                                Under 1973 Constitution of Pakistan Head of Government is ______?

                                Correct Answer: Prime Minister
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                • Under the 1973 Constitution of Pakistan, the Head of Government is the Prime Minister.

                                Additional information:

                                • The President serves as the Head of State with a ceremonial role.

                                Last verified on 10-02-2026

                                Pakistan secured a $7 billion IMF deal in 2024, with the first disbursement of ______?

                                Correct Answer: $1 billion
                                Show explanation

                                • Total IMF deal: $7 billion
                                • First disbursement: $1 billion
                                • Program length: 37 months
                                • Type: Extended Fund Facility (EFF)
                                • Year approved: 2024

                                Last verified on 10-02-2026

                                167

                                The 2025 India-Pakistan conflict began after a terrorist attack in the city _____?

                                Correct Answer: Pahalgam
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                • The 2025 India-Pakistan conflict began after a deadly terrorist attack in Pahalgam, Jammu & Kashmir on 22 April 2025.
                                • Militants attacked tourists in the Baisaran Valley near Pahalgam, killing 26 civilians.
                                • India blamed Pakistan-linked militant groups for the attack, which sharply escalated tensions.
                                • This led to India launching military strikes on Pakistan on 7 May 2025.

                                Last verified on 09-02-2026

                                168

                                The major environmental hazards in Sindh are _______?

                                Correct Answer: Salinity and waterlogging
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Environmental Hazards in Sindh

                                Major Issues:

                                • Salinity: Increase in salt content in soil, reducing agricultural productivity
                                • Waterlogging: Excess water accumulation due to poor drainage and irrigation practices

                                Causes: Irrigation mismanagement, high water table, and climate variations


                                Last verified on 08-02-2026

                                Correct Answer: Terrorism
                                Show explanation

                                National Action Plan (Pakistan)

                                • Formulated: After the 2014 Army Public School attack in Pakistan
                                • Purpose: To combat terrorism and extremist activities

                                An unexpected diplomatic handshake in late December 2025 between National Assembly speaker Ayaz Sadiq in Bangladesh, with ______?

                                Correct Answer: Jaishankar
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                • In late December 2025, Pakistan’s National Assembly Speaker Ayaz Sadiq met Indian External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar in Bangladesh.
                                • The meeting included an unexpected diplomatic handshake that drew media attention.
                                • It occurred during the SAARC parliamentary assembly in Dhaka, fostering regional dialogue.

                                171

                                In 2025, Pakistan evacuated thousands of families due to surging floods in ______?

                                Correct Answer: Punjab
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                • In 2025, severe monsoon floods in Pakistan displaced over 2 million people, mostly in Punjab.
                                • Flooding occurred in late August – early September.
                                • Rivers Ravi, Sutlej, Chenab overflowed due to heavy rains and dam releases from India.
                                • Major affected areas: Lahore, Narowal, Kasur, Sialkot, Multan.

                                Last verified on 08-02-2026

                                173

                                The overall literacy rate of Pakistan is ______?

                                Correct Answer: 63%
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The overall literacy rate of Pakistan is 62.3 % which means that around 90 million people in the country cannot read and write

                                literacy rate in Pakistan Province-wise data suggests that:

                                Punjab leads with 63%

                                Khyber Pakhtunkhwa with 53 %

                                Balochistan with 44 percent.has the lowest literacy rate in Pakistan

                                Islamabad has around 88% literacy rate, which is the highest rate in Pakistan.

                                • NA approval date: 21 Oct 2024 by President Asif Ali Zardari

                                175

                                Who abrogated 1962 constitution and became CMLA?

                                Correct Answer: Yahiya Khan
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                General Agha Muhammad Yahya Khan abrogated the 1962 Constitution of Pakistan 

                                And became the Chief Martial Law Administrator (CMLA) on March 25, 1969.

                                Additional information about the 1962 constitutions
                                • The presidential system of government was introduced 
                                • Urdu and Bengali were the official languages declared
                                • Ayoub Khan enforced the new constitution on 8th June 1962
                                • Iskandar Ali Mirza, the first president of Pakistan

                                Last updated 07-02-2026

                                176

                                Pakistan has launched military operation named ‘Operation Bunyan Um Marsoos’ against which country?

                                Correct Answer: India
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                • Operation Bunyan Um Marsoos was launched by Pakistan on 10 May 2025, after Fajr prayer.
                                • It targeted seven key Indian military sites, including Pathankot, Udhampur, Gujarat air bases, Rajasthan air base, and BrahMos storage site.
                                • The operation was retaliation for Indian missile strikes inside Pakistan.
                                • The name Bunyan Um Marsoos comes from Surah Al-Saff (61:4), meaning “a structure firmly joined together.”
                                • Verse reference: “Allah indeed loves those who fight in His Way as though they are a solid wall cemented with molten lead.”


                                Last verified on 07-02-2026

                                177

                                The legal framework order (LFO) 2002 was issued by _____?

                                Correct Answer: Pervaiz Musharraf
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The Legal Framework Order (LFO) was issued by two different individuals in Pakistan's history:

                                • 1. General Yahya Khan in 1970.
                                • 2. General Pervez Musharraf in 2002.

                                The first LFO was used to establish the legal framework for the 1970 general elections.

                                The second LFO, issued by Musharraf, made constitutional amendments and extended his presidential term.

                                Last verified on 07-02-2026

                                178

                                Who dissolved the assembly and imposed the First Martial Law in Pakistan?

                                Correct Answer: Iskandar Mirza
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                President Iskander Mirza dissolved the assembly and imposed the first martial law in Pakistan on October 7, 1958


                                List of Martial Laws Held in Pakistan from 1947 to 2022

                                Sr#

                                 Martial Law Number

                                Date of Martial Law

                                Martial Law Implemented by

                                1

                                First Martial Law

                                7th October 1958

                                Iskandar Mirza

                                2

                                2nd Martial Law

                                25th March 1969

                                General Yahya Khan

                                3

                                3rd Martial Law

                                25th July 1977

                                General Zia ul Haq

                                4

                                4th Martial Law

                                12th October 1999

                                General Pervaiz Musharraf

                                 

                                There are total four martial laws Implemented in Pakistan from 1947 to 2023

                                179

                                In which year West Pakistan become One Unit?

                                Correct Answer: 1955
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The One Unit was the reorganization of the provinces of Pakistan 


                                Prime Minister Muhammad Ali Bogra led it on 22 November 1954


                                Passed on 30 September 1955


                                This program merged the four provinces of West Pakistan into a single province to parallel the province of East Pakistan

                                • West Punjab
                                • Sind
                                • NWFP
                                • Baluchistan

                                Additional information:

                                The One Unit scheme was dissolved on July 1, 1970

                                General Yahya Khan imposed Legal Framework Order No. 1970 to end the program

                                and reinstate the provisional status of the Four Provinces as of August 1947.

                                180

                                How many volumes are there in Mirza Ghalib's Diwan?

                                Correct Answer: 5
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Mirza Ghalib's Diwan consists of 5 volumes.


                                These volumes compile his ghazals and other poetic works.


                                This showcasing his contributions to Urdu literature.

                                181

                                Which pact was signed between Pakistan and India after war of 1965?

                                Correct Answer: Tashkent Pact
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                The Tashkent Declaration was signed between India and Pakistan 

                                It was signed on 10 January 1966 to resolve the Indo-Pakistani War of 1965

                                It was a peace agreement 

                                Through the mediation of the Soviet Union 

                                It was signed by:
                                • President of Pakistan Ayub Khan 
                                • by India’s Prime Minister Lal Bahadur Shastri

                                It is four of the most popular romances.

                                183

                                The first census in Pakistan was held in?

                                Correct Answer: 1951
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The first census in Pakistan was held in 1951.


                                Additional information:

                                Total nationwide censuses in Pakistan: 7

                                • 1st Census: 1951
                                • 2nd Census: 1961
                                • 3rd Census: 1972
                                • 4th Census: 1981
                                • 5th Census: 1998
                                • 6th Census: 2017
                                • 7th Census: 2023

                                Frequency: Every 10 years (approx.)

                                Historical start: 1872 (British rule, first census in the region)

                                He was previously serving as vice chancellor of Quaid-e-Azam University.

                                185

                                Afforestation means ______?

                                Correct Answer: To convert land into forest
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                • Afforestation is the process of planting trees on land that was not previously forested.
                                  • Helps increase green cover and reduce soil erosion.
                                  • Important for climate change mitigation and biodiversity.
                                  • Commonly done in degraded, barren, or desert areas.
                                  • Supports environmental sustainability and carbon sequestration.

                                  Last verified on 06-02-2026

                                  186

                                  Older the rice ______?

                                  Correct Answer: Cheaper in price
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • Older rice refers to rice that has been stored for some time after harvest.
                                  • Its moisture content decreases, making it less sticky when cooked.
                                  • Often considered less fresh, so it is cheaper in price.
                                  • Popular for certain dishes like biryani due to better separation of grains.
                                  • Storage can improve aroma and cooking quality in some varieties.

                                  Last verified on 06-02-2026

                                  187

                                  The headquarters of Meezan Bank is located in which city?

                                  Correct Answer: Karachi
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation

                                  The headquarters of Meezan Bank is located in Karachi


                                  The bank's registered head office, known as Meezan House


                                  Meezan Bank was formerly known as Al Meezan Investment Bank.


                                  Renamed: Meezan Bank Limited (2002)


                                  Significance: It was the first Islamic commercial bank in Pakistan.


                                  Founded: 27 January 1997 


                                  Headquarters: Karachi


                                  In 2000: listed on the Karachi Stock Exchange.

                                  Correct Answer: 2005
                                  Show explanation

                                  Meezan Bank was incorporated on January 27, 1997


                                  Meezan Bank was formerly known as Al Meezan Investment Bank.


                                  Renamed: Meezan Bank Limited (2002)


                                  Significance: It was the first Islamic commercial bank in Pakistan.


                                  Headquarters: Karachi


                                  The Bank was granted a'Scheduled Islamic Commercial Bank' license on January 31, 2002

                                  Meezan Bank was formerly known as Al Meezan Investment Bank.


                                  Renamed: Meezan Bank Limited (2002)


                                  Significance: It was the first Islamic commercial bank in Pakistan.

                                  192

                                  Agricultural loan recovery mainly depends on?

                                  Correct Answer: Return capacity of loan
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation

                                  The recovery of an agricultural loan depends primarily on the repayment capacity (or return capacity) of the borrower. 


                                  Repayment of Ag loans depends primarily on the successful planting and harvest of crops (or the raising and feeding of livestock)


                                  Marketing the harvested commodity (including grain, milk, hay, cattle, swine, and poultry). 

                                  193

                                  Who appoints the Banking Mohtasib in Pakistan?

                                  Correct Answer: Federal Government
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation

                                  Federal Government (specifically, the President of Pakistan acting for the Federal Government)


                                  This appointment is made in consultation with the Governor of the State Bank of Pakistan. 


                                  Term of Office:  four years.

                                  194

                                  The Red Sindhi and Nili Ravi are breeds of which livestock in Pakistan?

                                  Correct Answer: Cattle (Cow and Buffalo)
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • Red Sindhi: Indigenous cow breed, reddish color, high milk yield, heat tolerant
                                  • Nili Ravi: Famous water buffalo breed, large body, high-fat milk production
                                  • Main Purpose: Dairy production for local and commercial use
                                  • Major Regions: Punjab, Sindh (Pakistan)
                                  • Adaptability: Well-suited to tropical and subtropical climates

                                  Last verified on 05-02-2025

                                  195

                                  Money circulation in Pakistan is controlled by?

                                  Correct Answer: State Bank of Pakistan
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation

                                  Money circulation in Pakistan is controlled by the State Bank of Pakistan (SBP)


                                  The State Bank of Pakistan (SBP) is the central bank of the country.


                                  It controls money supply, inflation, and circulation in the economy.

                                  196

                                  Loans are granted to farmers on what basis?

                                  Correct Answer: Repayment Capacity
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation

                                  Agricultural loans are mainly granted based on the farmer’s repayment capacity, i.e., their ability to repay the loan from crop income.


                                  Collateral, crop type, and farm size may be considered, but the primary criterion is repayment capacity as per State Bank of Pakistan guidelines.

                                  197

                                  Which one is the Kharif Crop among the following?

                                  Correct Answer: Cotton
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  Major Kharif Crops
                                  1. Rice
                                  2. Sugarcane
                                  3. Cotton
                                  4. Maize
                                  5. Bajra
                                  6. Jowar
                                  Additional information:
                                  Kharif Crop Period
                                  • Start: April – June
                                  • End: September – October
                                  Rabi Crop Period
                                  • Sowing: October – December
                                  • Harvesting: March – May

                                  Last verified on 25-01-2026

                                  198

                                  The economy of Pakistan is based on ______?

                                  Correct Answer: Agriculture
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • Agriculture contributes a major share to GDP (around one-fifth).
                                    • Provides raw material to textile and sugar industries.
                                    • Employs a large portion of the rural population.
                                    • Major crops: Wheat, Cotton, Rice, Sugarcane, Maize.
                                    • Irrigation mainly comes from the Indus River system.

                                    Last verified on 05-02-2026

                                    199

                                    Who is the Current Governor of the State Bank of Pakistan (SBP)?

                                    Correct Answer: Jameel Ahmed
                                    Show explanation
                                    Explanation
                                    • Name: Jameel Ahmad
                                    • Term: 5 years, 19 August 2022
                                    • Previously, Murtaza Syed 


                                    ======

                                    History of State Bank

                                      • The President of Pakistan appoints the Governor.
                                      • Term of five years.
                                      • Zahid Husain was the founder and the first governor of the State Bank of Pakistan from June 1948 to July 1953.
                                      • Dr. Shamshad Akhtar became the first female Governor of State Bank of Pakistan in 2006.

                                      Last updated by NS on 06 May 2026


                                         



                                      200

                                      Kashmir Solidarity Day is celebrated in Pakistan on which date?

                                      Correct Answer: February 5
                                      Show explanation
                                      Explanation

                                      Kashmir Solidarity Day or Kashmir Day is a national holiday observed in Pakistan 

                                      Date: February 5

                                      Kashmir Day Event Length: 1 Day

                                      Kashmir Day Related to the Kashmir Conflict

                                      Kashmir Day was Started by: Jamaat-e-Islami Pakistan in 1990

                                      Kashmir Day was first proposed by Qazi Hussain Ahmad 

                                      In 1991, the then-Prime Minister of Pakistan Nawaz Sharif called for a "Kashmir Solidarity Day Strike"


                                      Nice work getting this far. Consistency beats intensity — come back tomorrow for the next lesson.
